claim_boundary.next_gate: If unseen-seed robustness fails, diagnose group-level route variance and expand the candidate objective without opening the verifier holdout.
claim_boundary.what_is_not_supported: Verifier holdout performance, readout mitigation, current calibration, provider access, hardware execution, protocol soundness, quantum advantage, BQP separation, or new B10 credit.
claim_boundary.what_is_supported: Disjoint-seed validation of lower-tail transpiler layout ranking over the 60 predeclared R127 candidates on frozen snapshots.

requirements.label: R127 and R128 sources are hash-bound before R129 selection
training and unseen validation transpiler seeds are disjoint
all 60 retained candidates are trained on all eight training seeds
selection uses lower-tail paired gain before mean exposure
ten unseen seeds are evaluated for selected, default, and R128 layouts
all 60 selected validation QASM artifacts preserve measurement order
the robust unseen-seed gate is evaluated on all six groups
R125 acceptance rows, verifier holdout, and mitigation remain excluded
historical snapshots remain separate from current and hardware evidence
no soundness, advantage, BQP, or new credit is claimed
